what_it_does: A posted note/comment by Tim in the dragnet-development channel, responding to another noticeboard item about the iOS keyboard space-bar trackpad as a precedent for a hold-to-enter-mode, move-to-scrub, lift-to-commit interaction primitive.
observations: Tim endorses the iOS spacebar trackpad precedent as 'immediately cool'
He states he uses the feature every day but did not know about it consciously
He acknowledges it proves the exact primitive already lives in his muscle memory
He praises the design research for surfacing precedents he would not have specified
It is filed as a comment on item-47cb5563
Authored by tim; type note; state posted; channel dragnet-development
standouts: Tim validates a precedent that he already performs daily but could not have articulated himself
Explicit praise for design research that discovers better references than the author's own knowledge
notes: Tim's enthusiastic validation that the iOS spacebar trackpad captures a hold-move-lift primitive already embedded in his daily muscle memory.

intention_signals: To capture and broadcast Tim's positive reception of the iOS spacebar trackpad precedent, signaling that precedents grounded in his unconscious daily behavior are especially valuable.
novelty: Frames a routine iOS feature as a formal interaction primitive and highlights that the strongest precedents may be ones the designer already performs but cannot specify.
contribution: A first-person validation that the hold-move-lift primitive is rooted in existing daily muscle memory, reinforcing the relevance of the cited precedent for the design substrate.
